basic_details.job_title: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 779 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Candidates searching for the BOI Credit Officer Vacancy 2026 details can refer to the quick summary below for an instant overview of the post, organization, vacancy count, last date, and application mode.

application_fee.details: Candidates applying for the BOI Credit Officer Recruitment Apply Online 2026 must pay the application fee online between 30 June and 20 July 2026. The fee differs by category as shown below. | Payment can be made using Debit/Credit Cards, Internet Banking, UPI, or Mobile Wallets. Bank transaction charges, if any, will be borne by the candidate.

age_limit.details: Post Minimum Age Maximum Age MMGS-II 25 years 32 years MMGS-III 28 years 38 years SMGS-IV 32 years 45 years | Age relaxation is applicable as follows: 5 years for SC/ST , 3 years for OBC (Non-creamy layer) , 10 years for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities , and 5 years for Ex-Servicemen and persons affected by the 1984 riots.

qualification.details: Candidates checking eligibility for the Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 should note that qualification and experience requirements vary by grade. A graduation degree with 60% marks (55% for SC/ST/OBC/PWBD) or professional qualifications like CA/CMA-ICWA/CS/CFA is required, along with post-qualification banking/credit experience. | Age relaxation is applicable as follows: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (Non-creamy layer), 10 years for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities, and 5 years for Ex-Servicemen and persons affected by the 1984 riots. | A Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from recognized University/Institute with 60% marks or equivalent grade (55% for SC/ ST/ OBC/ PWBD) recognized by the Govt. of India or any equivalent qualification recognized as such by the Central Government or CA/CMA-ICWA/CS/CFA

salary_details.details: Candidates wondering about the salary structure for the Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 can refer to the pay scale below for each grade. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: Selection for the BOI Credit Officer Bharti 2026 will be through an Online Examination and/or Personal Interview
depending on the number of applicants. If both stages are conducted
the weightage will be 70:30 between the Online Examination and Interview.
Scrutiny of Eligibility: Shortlisting based on documents uploaded at registration. Online Examination: 125 marks total - English Language (25 marks
qualifying nature) and Professional Knowledge (100 marks)
composite duration of 120 minutes. Personal Interview: 100 marks
conducted for candidates shortlisted based on merit. Final Merit List: Prepared based on combined scores of Online Examination and Interview.
The minimum qualifying marks in each test is 35% for General/EWS candidates
with a 5% relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PWD candidates. There is a penalty of one-fourth marks for each wrong answer in the objective test.

all_official_links.label: Download Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 PDF

faq.question: Q1. Has Bank of India officially released the Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 notification?
faq.answer: Yes, Bank of India released the official notification for Credit Officer (GBO Stream) Recruitment 2026 on 01 June 2026 under Project No. 2026-27/01.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available in Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 779 total vacancies across MMGS-II (677), MMGS-III (61), and SMGS-IV (41) grades.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹175 for SC/ST/PWD candidates and ₹850 for General and other category candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for Bank of India Credit Officer posts?
faq.answer: Age limits vary by grade: 25-32 years for MMGS-II, 28-38 years for MMGS-III, and 32-45 years for SMGS-IV, as on 01 June 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through an Online Examination and/or Personal Interview, with a 70:30 weightage if both stages are conducted.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for Bank of India Credit Officer posts?
faq.answer: Pay scales range from ₹64,820 for MMGS-II up to ₹1,20,940 for SMGS-IV, along with other allowances.

faq.question: Q8. What is the minimum qualification required for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates need a graduation degree with 60% marks (55% for SC/ST/OBC/PWBD) or CA/CMA-ICWA/CS/CFA, along with relevant credit/banking experience.

faq.question: Q9. How can candidates apply for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through www.bankofindia.bank.in under the Career section from 30 June 2026 to 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q10. What is the security deposit required for selected candidates?
faq.answer: Selected candidates must deposit ₹2,00,000 (MMGS-II) or ₹3,00,000 (MMGS-III/SMGS-IV), refundable after 3 years of satisfactory service.
